title: AAIB Investigates ATR 72-212 A Diversion at Cornwall Airport

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content:
The Air Accident Investigations Branch (AAIB) has launched an investigation into an incident involving an ATR 72-212 A aircraft, registered as G-IACZ. The aircraft was diverted on 15 April 2025, as it could not fully extend its flaps during the approach to Cornwall Airport, Newquay.

The AAIB's inquiry aims to determine the circumstances surrounding the technical issue that led to the diversion of the aircraft. Diversions due to mechanical malfunctions emphasize the importance of safety protocols in aviation. With roots in a rich history of aviation, including developments from companies like De Havilland and the contributions of Scottish engineers, the UK's aviation sector prioritises safety and reliability in all air travel operations.
